generated_oml:
// C1: Physical Sensing Components
instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Post-occupancy occupancy sensors embedded within the building, feeding real-time occupancy data into the DT via Eclipse Hono middleware"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyData
]

instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental temperature sensors measuring temperature/humidity within the building"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureData
]

instance HumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental humidity sensors measuring relative humidity levels in the building"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalParametersData
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Energy consumption sensors tracking power usage metrics from building systems"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyData
]

instance LightLevelSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Light intensity sensors measuring ambient lighting conditions for adaptive control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightData
]

// Data Transmitted from Sensing Components
instance OccupancyData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
    base:desc "Real-time occupancy status data transmitted via Eclipse Hono protocol"
]

instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
    base:desc "Real-time temperature readings transmitted via Ditto middleware"
]

instance EnvironmentalParametersData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor, HumiditySensor
    base:desc "Comprehensive environmental parameters combining temperature and humidity data"
]

instance EnergyData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
    base:desc "Real-time energy consumption metrics transmitted via Hono protocol"
]

instance LightData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightLevelSensor
    base:desc "Real-time light intensity measurements transmitted via Ditto protocol"
]

// Data Models
instance BIMEnrichedModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Enriched BIM models containing static geometry, topology, and functional attributes extended with runtime semantics via MDE"
]

instance OperationalStateModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic 3D visualization model in Unreal Engine representing real-time operational state of the building"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngineVisualizer
]

// Data
instance OccupancyOccurrenceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Occupancy occurrence data derived from post-occupancy sensor readings"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupancyData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AdaptiveLightingController
]

instance EnvironmentalParametersData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Comprehensive environmental parameters combining temperature and humidity data"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnergyOptimizationService
]

// Enablers/Tooling
instance BIMMetadataExtractor : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering tool that automates metadata extraction from BIM models for sensor endpoint configuration"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoMiddleware, DittoConfigurator
]

instance RuntimeSemanticsGenerator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MDE component that extends BIM models with runtime components (sensors, actuators, control logic)"
]

instance EclipseHonoMiddleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data exchange protocol for bidirectional communication between physical and virtual building components"
    DTDFVocab:enables AdaptiveLightingController, EnergyOptimizationService
]

instance DittoConfigurator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware configurator that extracts metadata (tenant/device IDs) and generates runtime artifacts"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMMetadataExtractor
]

instance UnrealEngineVisualizer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D visualization platform for interactive building state representation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo OperationalStateModel
]

// Physical Acting Components
instance TenantLightActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Lighting control devices configured per tenant within the building automation system"
]

instance BuildingControlSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Centralized building control system integrating tenant-specific configurations from BIM"
]

instance AdaptiveLightingController : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Adaptive lighting control system using reinforcement learning to balance energy consumption, visual comfort, and non-visual health effects"
    DTDFVocab:provides DynamicLightingAdjustment, ComfortOptimization
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoMiddleware
]

instance EnergyOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Energy optimization service leveraging BIM semantics to automate control logic configuration"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlPolicyGeneration, PredictiveMaintenance
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoMiddleware
]

// Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring and visualization platform using Unreal Engine for immersive 3D interaction with building operational state"
    DTDFVocab:provides OccupantComfortVisualization, EnergyUsageDashboard
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngineVisualizer
]

// Insights/Actions
instance OccupantComfortVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D visualization of occupant comfort metrics derived from real-time environmental data"
]

instance EnergyUsageDashboard : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard showing real-time energy consumption patterns and efficiency metrics"
]

instance DynamicLightingAdjustment : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of lighting levels based on occupancy patterns and environmental conditions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ComfortOptimization : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Optimization of non-visual health effects through adaptive lighting control policies"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlPolicyGeneration : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of control policies using reinforcement learning algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PredictiveMaintenance : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance scheduling based on operational data trends"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// Environment/Component Containers
instance BuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains OccupancySensor, TemperatureSensor, HumiditySensor,
             EnergyConsumptionSensor, LightLevelSensor,
             TenantLightActuator, BuildingControlSystem
]

instance DTFrameworkHub : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains BIMEnrichedModel, OperationalStateModel,
             EclipseHonoMiddleware, DittoConfigurator,
             UnrealEngineVisualizer, AdaptiveLightingController,
             EnergyOptimizationService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** occurs via **control signals** generated by the DT’s services (e.g., adaptive lighting control, energy optimization policies) and transmitted back to the building’s BCS. This is enabled by **Eclipse Hono/Ditto middleware**, which supports bidirectional communication between the DT and physical actuators (e.g., lighting dimmers, HVAC systems). The framework leverages **Unreal Engine** for visualization and simulation, where control logic is iteratively refined (e.g., via reinforcement learning in BOREALIS) to optimize operational performance."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The DT operates on a **real-time synchronization** time scale, with sub-second latency for data exchange between physical assets and the virtual model. The middleware (Eclipse Hono/Ditto) ensures live synchronization of sensor data and control signals, enabling near-real-time decision-making for operational optimization."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is orchestrated as a **service-oriented, modular ecosystem** composed of:
  1. A **centralized DT framework** (hosted on the BIM platform) that automates device modeling, runtime configuration, and service integration.
  2. **Decentralized DT components** (e.g., separate twins for lighting, HVAC, energy systems) connected via **Eclipse Hono/Ditto middleware** for interoperability.
  3. **Stakeholder roles** (Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers) interacting via standardized APIs to deploy, monitor, and extend DT functionality.
  The architecture ensures **scalability** and **interoperability** across heterogeneous AECO systems, addressing fragmentation in current DT deployments."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT spans the following **life-cycle stages** of the building:
  1. **Design phase**: Enriched BIM models incorporate static and dynamic BCS components (e.g., sensors, actuators) via model-driven transformations.
  2. **Construction phase**: The framework automates the extraction of device metadata from BIM for real-time deployment (e.g., sensor endpoint configuration).
  3. **Operation phase**: Continuous synchronization via **Eclipse Hono/Ditto** ensures the DT reflects the building’s operational state, enabling real-time monitoring, control, and optimization (e.g., adaptive lighting via BOREALIS).
  4. **Refurbishment phase**: The modular architecture supports dynamic updates and service integration without redeployment, ensuring the DT evolves with the building’s lifecycle."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  1. **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric**: Quantifies the alignment between the DT and its physical counterpart, validating real-time synchronization.
  2. **Model-driven transformations**: Automatically extract and validate BIM metadata (e.g., sensor configurations) to reduce semantic drift.
  3. **Runtime synchronization**: Eclipse Hono/Ditto middleware guarantees low-latency, bidirectional data exchange, minimizing discrepancies between virtual and physical states.
  4. **Verification/validation**: The framework’s automated pipeline reduces manual effort, while iterative updates (e.g., BOREALIS reinforcement learning) enhance operational accuracy."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin employs a **middleware-based architecture** using Eclipse Hono and Ditto for bidirectional communication. Technical connections rely on MQTT/REST protocols to transmit sensor data (e.g., temperature, occupancy) from physical devices to the DT and control signals (e.g., lighting adjustments) from the DT to Building Control Systems. The system ensures low-latency, reliable communication, critical for real-time synchronization."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is deployed using a **hybrid middleware-based architecture**, where runtime components (e.g., Hono/Ditto) handle real-time data exchange. Deployment is not explicitly cloud/edge-specific in the documents; however, the focus is on middleware integration for scalability and interoperability."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration is achieved through:
  1. **Standardized extension points**: Enable modular service integration with other DTs or external systems (e.g., IoT platforms).
  2. **Interoperability frameworks**: The DT Framework supports seamless communication with legacy systems and BIM tools via middleware (Eclipse Hono/Ditto)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to the following standards/methodologies:
  1. **Building Information Modeling (BIM)**: Semantic backbone for building representation and data integration.
  2. **Model-driven Engineering (MDE)**: Automates transformations and ensures consistency across life-cycle stages.
  3. **Policy initiatives and regulatory mandates**: General support for BIM adoption in AECO (e.g., ISO standards implied but not explicitly named)."
]
